title: Universal Intermediate Representation
description: Ecosystem-agnostic capture of a repo's AI assistant requirements. All ecosystem generators read from this; all parsers write to it.

scope.description: When this rule applies. 'global' = always; 'file-glob' = only on matched files; 'directory' = scoped to a path prefix; 'language' = scoped to a language; 'task-type' = scoped to a class of AI task

scope_value.description: For 'file-glob': '**/*.test.ts'. For 'directory': 'src/api/'. For 'language': 'typescript'. For 'task-type': 'commit-message' | 'code-review' | 'refactor' | 'documentation'. Null for 'global'.

priority.description: 1 = critical (always enforce, alwaysApply in Cursor). 5 = standard convention. 10 = suggestion. Used to drive alwaysApply vs manual activation in Cursor.

category.description: Semantic category for grouping and filtering during generation

content.description: The rule text in plain English. Single responsibility. Written as an instruction to the AI.

rationale.description: Why this rule exists. Included in rich-format ecosystems (Claude Code, Cursor). Omitted in flat-text ecosystems.

examples.description: Optional code examples illustrating the rule

tags.description: Free-form tags for filtering and cross-referencing

_degraded.description: Set to true during translation when this rule lost fidelity from source ecosystem

_degraded_reason.description: Human-readable explanation of what was lost during translation

description.description: Activation trigger text for Claude Code. Used as section header in other ecosystems.

instructions.description: Full markdown instruction body for the skill

scope.description: File glob this skill primarily applies to, if any

tools_allowed.description: Tool names or patterns this agent is allowed to use

scope.description: Directory or glob this agent is responsible for

matcher.description: Tool name or glob pattern to match for PreToolUse/PostToolUse. Null to match all.

command.description: Shell command to execute when this hook fires

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this hook does

meta.description: Identity and provenance of this UIR document

id.description: UUID v4 for this UIR instance. Stable identifier for caching and change detection.

version.description: Semver of this UIR document (not the project). Increment minor on field additions, major on breaking schema changes.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this UIR was produced

method.description: 'analyze' = auto-generated from repo scan; 'manual' = hand-written; 'translate' = converted from another ecosystem

repo_path.description: Absolute or relative path to the analyzed repo when method is 'analyze'

context.description: What the AI should know about the project at all times. Maps to CLAUDE.md header, Cursor index.mdc, Codex AGENTS.md header, Aider CONVENTIONS.md.

project_summary.description: 1–3 paragraph description of the project: what it does, who it's for, and its tech stack. Rendered at the top of all config files.

tech_stack.description: Ordered list of technologies (most important first)

directory_layout.description: Key directories mapped to their purpose. E.g. { 'src/': 'Application source', 'tests/': 'Test suite', 'infra/': 'IaC definitions' }

entry_points.description: Main files, commands, or scripts to understand the project. E.g. ['src/index.ts', 'docker-compose.yml']

monorepo.description: Present when this is a monorepo. Null for single-package repos.

packages.description: Package/app paths relative to repo root

rules.description: Core behavioral rules for AI assistants. Unique by id. All ecosystem generators consume this array.

permissions.description: What the AI agent is allowed or forbidden to do. Native to Claude Code settings.json. Degrades to rule text in other ecosystems.

allow.description: Tool/action patterns the agent may execute without confirmation. E.g. 'Bash(git *)', 'Read', 'Write(**/*.ts)'

ask_on.description: Patterns that require user confirmation. Claude Code-native. Degrades to 'always ask before X' rule text in other ecosystems.

memory.description: Persistent context the AI should maintain across sessions

always_load.description: File paths (relative to repo root) to always include in AI context. Maps to CLAUDE.md @imports, Cursor alwaysApply rules, Aider --read.

on_demand.description: File paths to load when topically relevant. Maps to Cursor manual-activation rules, Aider /add.

ignore.description: Glob patterns to exclude from AI context. Merged into ignore_patterns during generation.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks — shell commands triggered by agent events. Native to Claude Code. Degrades to rule text in Cursor, dropped in Codex/Aider.

skills.description: Reusable task skill definitions. Claude Code-native via Skill tool. Translated to Cursor manual-activation .mdc rules or Aider CONVENTIONS.md sections.

agents.description: Sub-agent definitions. Maps to AGENTS.md for both Claude Code and OpenAI Codex CLI. Flattened to context section in Cursor and Aider.

ignore_patterns.description: Unified ignore list in gitignore syntax. Rendered to .aiderignore, .cursorignore, and respected by Claude Code.

extensions.description: Ecosystem-specific data that cannot be represented in UIR core fields. Keyed by ecosystem id. Generators merge these into output but validators flag unknown keys.
